reasoning: The Wikipedia article 'FIDE rankings' tracks the official standard Elo rating lists released by FIDE on the 1st of every month. The March 2026 ratings (which give Alina Kashlinskaya a rating of 2480) have already been published and added to Wikipedia as of early March (I verified the revision from March 4). Consequently, the value on the Wikipedia page on March 15 will be her March 1 rating (2480). Because FIDE does not release new standard rating lists until April 1, the Wikipedia page will remain unchanged on March 22. Even in the highly unlikely event of a live rating being used, a 1% jump (about 25 points) in a week is exceptionally difficult at her level. Thus, the rating on 2026-03-22 will almost certainly be exactly the same as on 2026-03-15 (0% difference).
